mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the equity discount rate and reduce the present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS level fails to show negative Rank IC with 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window (IC > 0 or |t-stat| < 2).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes consumer spending declines and negative earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC is positive in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive momentum in consumer prices and negative momentum in payroll employment signals stagflationary pressure that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint conditioning of CPIAUCSL and PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of active periods, or walk-forward Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts predict positive S&P 500 returns during monetary easing cycles because lower mortgage rates amplify construction demand and upstream industrial orders; during tightening cycles this transmission is severed and the signal is nullified.
failure_condition: HOUST momentum produces positive 60-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of observations during months when FEDFUNDS is below its 12-month trailing average.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth captures real output expansion and capacity utilization improvements that translate into earnings surprises, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month change fails to achieve positive Rank IC > 0.03 with 5-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp three-month decline in retail sales signals collapsing consumer demand that forces negative revenue revisions across consumer-facing sectors, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in payroll employment and industrial production confirms a synchronized economic expansion that lifts aggregate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and INDPRO fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of joint-acceleration months, or walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month consumer price index spike above 0.4% surprises markets and triggers a rapid repricing of real discount rates and forward earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances following the CPIAUCSL trigger, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
